rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT WHITE ANBIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed: (1) The Court to determine whether the will instructs that Patricia and Charline are the residual beneficiaries. (2) Need clarification on the calculation of the fee base. The total of the I&A values was $1,316,000, plus $524,732.76, which equals $ 1,840,732.76. When there are losses from the sale of the real property-the difference between $1,310,000 and $1,050,000, which is $260,000-the amount should be adjusted to $1,580,732.76. This is calculated as $1,840,732.76 minus $260,000, resulting in $1,580,732.76. Therefore, the correct statutory fee appears to be $28,807.33. (3) The attorney's declaration does not meet CRC 7.702. Most services performed appear to be ordinary administration-related services. (4) Proposed distribution must include the amount of cash to be distributed to each recipient. "50%" is insufficient. CRC 7.651 and LRSF 14.35.I. (5) Clarification of the cost advanced by counsel. Petition #26 requests reimbursement of $2,704.10; however, Exhibit G only shows $2,570.35. (6) The above issues (except for #1 and #3) must be cleared by the petitioner's verified document. (7) The proposed order must be revised accordingly, and its caption must begin with Order for Final Distribution. LRSF 14.35.I.6).b. (8) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF MIGUEL ANTONIO CINTO MEJIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion (Cal Rule Of Court 3.110) And Motion For Nunc Pro Tunc Execution Of Specvial Juvenil Immigrant Findings On Gc224 Per Previous Order
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Motion for Nunc Pro Tunc Execution of Special Juvenile Immigrant Findings on GC-224 Per Previous Order ("Motion"). On June 26, 2024, this court entered Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224), as prepared and submitted to the court by counsel for the petitioner and ward, Miguel Antonio Cinto Mejia. Per the Motion, the findings at paragraph 5 contain two typographical errors in the first sentence of the second paragraph in the section. The sentence currently reads as follows: "PARENTAL ABENDONMENT: THE COURT FINDS that there is evidence to support findings that the ward, Erick, was abandoned by his parents while living in El Salvador." The name "Erick" and the location "El Salvador" are scrivener's errors. Movant has submitted a proposed Amended Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224) with the correct sentence. The function of a nunc pro tunc order is to correct the record; not to make an order now for then, but to enter now for then an order previously made. (In re Careaga's Estate (1964) 61 Cal.2d 471, 474.) "The question that is presented to the court on a hearing of a motion for a nunc pro tunc order is: What order was in fact made at the time by the trial judge?" (Id.) Here, Judge Moody is the judicial officer who signed the subject Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224). He remembers the hearing and what he intended to find and order. The first sentence of the second paragraph at paragraph 5 of the June 26, 2024 Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224), is hereby corrected nunc pro tunc, to read as follows: "PARENTAL ABENDONMENT: THE COURT FINDS that there is evidence to support findings that the ward, Miguel, was abandoned by his parents while living in Guatemala." The Court enters this order nunc pro tunc to correct the June 26, 2024 Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224) so as to comport with Judge Moody's intent, effective to the date of entry of the June 26, 2024 Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224). The court will issue Amended Special Immigrant Juvenile Findings (form GC-224) concurrently with this Order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BLANCHE GREUTERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) Pecuniary bequests not distributed within one year after the testator's death bear interest. [Probate Code Sections 12003 and 12001.] (4) The petition does not appear to address the specific gift to Anthony Shu. (5) It appears that funds well in excess of the FDIC insured amount are held at one bank. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/19/26. An amendment to the petition must be filed to correct the proposed distribution to the four charities and, therefore, to the residuary beneficiaries. A courtesy copy of the petition was not provided to the examiner, so it was only cursorily reviewed online. It appears that receipts used in calculating the fee base included refunds and the calculation of the statutory fee was also slightly off. After the amendment to the petition is e-filed, notice of it must be given. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SACHIN SHASHIKANT VORA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/2/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) still has not been filed. Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (2) Whose parents were Himmatlal P. Vora and Savita H. Vora? Did they have only one child, presumably Shashikant H. Vora? (3) Who were Renuka S. Vora's parents? (4) In other words, who were Mrudula Bharat Shah's and Nila Lalit Shah's parent(s)? (5) What is the petitioner's relationship to the decedent? "Petitioner" is not a relationship; one is either kin or a stranger. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/17/26. Additional information must be provided in a SUPPLEMENT to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner. It might help if a family tree were provided so that the relationships of ALL potential heirs of the decedent are clear. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. A Judicial Council form must not be used for a supplement to a pleading. [CRC 7.104(b)(2).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE F THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Petitioner Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. To Will Contest Of Bin Bin Thomas
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Demurrer of Petitioner Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. to Will Contest of Bin Bin Thomas. Request for Judicial Notice of certain court records pursuant to Evidence Code sections 451(a) and 452(d) is GRANTED. Per the Notice of Demurrer, Movant demurs to Contest and Grounds of Objection to Probate of Purported Will, filed on May 12, 2025 ("Will Contest"), on two grounds: 1. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(b), in that Bin Bin Thomas "does not have legal capacity" to bring a will contest and objection to probate of will in this matter because she is not an "interested person" under Probate Code section 48; and 2. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e), in that the Will Contest fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. The MPA discusses the demurring party's assertion that Ms. Thomas is not an "interested person" under the Probate Code. Petitioner's Demurrer to the Will Contest on these grounds is OVERRULED, as follows. A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading, based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 996, 1008, 1009.) The court notes, judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) .) "A court may take judicial notice of the existence of each document in a court file, but can only take judicial notice of the truth of facts asserted in documents such as orders, findings of fact and conclusions of law, and judgments. [citation.] [ ] A mistaken notion exists that taking judicial notice of court records means noticing " '... the existence of facts asserted in [all the documents in] a court file ... a court cannot take judicial notice of hearsay allegations as being true, just because they are part of a court record or file....' " (Garcia v. Sterling (1985) 176 Cal. App. 3d 17, 21-22, quoting Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914, emphasis in original.) Exhibit C to the Request for Judicial Notice is the Judgment for Dissolution entered on December 27, 2023 in George Thomas v. Bin Bin Thomas (FDI-23-798582), referenced in both the Will Contest and the Demurrer. This is the only document on this Demurrer where the court can take judicial notice of the truth of the facts asserted therein, and not merely judicial notice of the document's existence. The MPA argues, with citation to statutes and case law, that although Decedent died before the date the Judgment states the marriage would terminate, Ms. Thomas is not a "surviving spouse" or an "interested person" under the Probate Code. The Will Contest is facially pleaded in such a way so as to anticipate a challenge to Ms. Thomas' standing to bring it. At paragraph 2, the Will Contest pleads: "Contestant is Bin Bin Thomas, Decedent/Settlor George Thomas' wife from January 8, 2017. She was still married to him upon his death on January 1, 2024." At paragraph 8, the Will Contest pleads: "Contestant is a person interested in the devolution of Decedent's estate and entitled to contest admission of decedent's purported will to probate for the following reasons in that Contestant is Decedent's is [sic] surviving spouse, and is Decedent's sole intestate heir-at-law. As an intestate heir she has standing to contest admission of the will to probate."

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE F THOMAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] The Will Contest is facially pleaded to address the Judgment for Dissolution and its purported effect on Ms. Thomas' standing in this matter, at 3:22-27: "Although the Court entered the decree of dissolution of the marriage, George died on January 1, 2024, before the divorce became final. Bin Bin is confident that the divorce can be set aside. Once that is done, she will regain her status as George's widow, and his omitted spouse." Unlike the case cited in the MPA, the Will Contest facially challenges the validity of the dissolution proceedings as a product of Respondent's undue influence and wrongdoing against Decedent and Ms. Thomas, at 3:9-22 (see, Est. of McDaniel (2008) 161 Cal. App. 4th 458, 460, "Thereafter neither party challenged the judgment or its legal effect."). When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.; Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) Cal.4th 992, 998.) Demurrers test legal sufficiency based on the allegations as pleaded, without regard to the plaintiff's ability to prove the alleged facts. (see, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) A ruling on a demurrer does not determine contested factual issues relating to the merits. The sole question raised by demurrer is whether the pleading is facially-not factually-deficient. Whether Ms. Thomas will be able to meet the relevant burden of proof on the Will Contest's factual allegations regarding her standing as Decedent's surviving spouse or otherwise as an "interested person" under the Probate Code, is not the issue on a demurrer. Accepting as true all material facts pleaded in the Will Contest and those arising by reasonable implication, and matters that are judicially noticeable, as the court must do for the purpose of the demurrer, the Will Contest survives the Demurrer brought under Code of Civil Procedure sections 430.10(b) and (e), on the basis of lack of standing. A responsive pleading to the Will Contest shall be filed and served to within thirty (30) days of entry of this order. Ms. Thomas shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 05/03/2027.
